title: Running AI Agents in Automated Workflows: What If Your Documentation Wrote Itself?
description: Intility describes a reusable GitHub Action workflow that leverages Claude Code to automatically generate and update documentation whenever pull requests are merged, eliminating manual documentation maintenance with a security-first architecture.

title: Enhanced Agentic-RAG: What If Chatbots Could Deliver Near-Human Precision?
description: Uber's engineering team built EAg-RAG to improve Genie, their internal on-call copilot. The agentic workflow achieved a 27% increase in acceptable answers and a 60% reduction in incorrect advice across security and privacy channels.

title: Building production-ready agentic systems: Lessons from Shopify Sidekick
description: Shopify engineers share architectural insights from developing Sidekick, including Just-in-Time instructions for managing 50+ tools, LLM evaluation frameworks calibrated to human judgment, and Group Relative Policy Optimization training.

title: GitHub Copilot: Meet the new coding agent
description: GitHub introduces an asynchronous coding agent that takes GitHub issues, spins up a secure dev environment via GitHub Actions, writes code, runs tests, and opens draft pull requests — excelling at low-to-medium complexity tasks in well-tested codebases.

title: Klarna AI assistant handles two-thirds of customer service chats in its first month
description: Klarna's OpenAI-powered AI assistant handled 2.3 million conversations in its first month, doing the work of 700 full-time agents. Resolution time dropped from 11 minutes to under 2 minutes with customer satisfaction on par with human agents.

title: 1,500+ PRs Later: Spotify's Journey with Our Background Coding Agent
description: Spotify built an internal CLI leveraging LLMs to automate complex code migrations and maintenance tasks across their Fleet Management platform, generating over 1,500 merged pull requests with 60–90% time savings compared to manual implementation.

title: Our Multi-Agent Architecture for Smarter Advertising
description: Spotify developed Ads AI, a multi-agent system using Google's Agent Development Kit and Vertex AI with specialized agents working in parallel to generate optimized media plans, reducing planning time from 15–30 minutes to seconds.

title: The LinkedIn Generative AI Application Tech Stack: Extending to Build AI Agents
description: LinkedIn describes how they extended their GenAI platform to build multi-agent systems, including their architecture for orchestrating AI agents with human-in-the-loop controls, observability via LangSmith and OpenTelemetry, and MCP adoption for interoperability.

title: Building Dash: How RAG and AI agents help us meet the needs of businesses
description: Dropbox built Dash, a universal search platform combining RAG and AI agents to help knowledge workers find information across fragmented apps. Features a custom Python interpreter for secure code execution and delivers results in under two seconds for 95% of queries.

title: How Dash uses context engineering for smarter AI
description: Dropbox describes how Dash evolved from traditional search into an agentic AI platform, solving context overload by consolidating retrieval tools, filtering through a knowledge graph, and delegating complex tasks to specialized agents.

title: AI-Assisted Development at Block
description: Block deployed AI agents across its 12,000-person workforce in eight weeks. 95% of engineers now use AI assistance regularly, with most managing multiple parallel agents. Their open-source framework Goose connects LLMs to real-world actions like checking code and orchestrating tools.

title: From resolutions to outcomes: Evolving how Fin delivers value
description: Intercom shares how Fin's AI agent evolved beyond binary resolution metrics. With a 76% average resolution rate, they're transitioning to measuring outcomes — recognizing that optimal results sometimes require Fin to collaborate with human agents on complex cases.

title: How we built our multi-agent research system
description: Anthropic describes the engineering behind their multi-agent Research feature, where a planning agent decomposes complex queries and spawns parallel search agents. The post covers architectural principles, prompting strategies, and evaluation methods for reliable multi-agent systems.

title: 30.8% Faster PRs: How AI-Driven Rovo Dev Code Reviewer Improved Developer Productivity at Atlassian
description: Atlassian's year-long evaluation across 1,900+ repositories shows Rovo Dev reduced PR cycle time by 30.8% and manual review comments by 35.6%. Built with Claude 3.5 Sonnet using a three-stage quality control pipeline with human-in-the-loop authority.

title: Inside Replit's Snapshot Engine: The Tech Making AI Agents Safe
description: Replit built a snapshot engine using Copy-on-Write technology to create isolated, reversible environments for AI agents. Instant filesystem forks, versioned databases, and sandboxed execution let developers experiment without risking permanent damage.

title: The AI engineering stack we built internally — on the platform we ship
description: Cloudflare built their internal AI infrastructure on their own products, processing 241 billion tokens through AI Gateway for 3,683 employees. The iMARS team deployed 13 MCP servers exposing 182+ tools across GitLab, Jira, Sentry, and more.

title: Streamlining Security Investigations with Agents
description: Slack's Security Engineering team deployed a multi-persona agent system — Director, Expert, and Critic agents working together — that performed over 7,500 security investigations and 500,000 tool calls in its first quarter, with structured outputs reducing hallucinations.

title: Introducing Notion 3.0
description: Notion rebuilt their AI from the ground up as autonomous Agents capable of 20+ minutes of multi-step work across pages — creating documents, building databases, and executing workflows. Custom Agents let teams automate domain-specific processes at scale.

title: Introducing the Agentic Commerce Suite: A complete solution for selling on AI agents
description: Stripe launched a platform for businesses to sell through AI agents with a single integration — handling product discovery, checkout, and payments. Features fraud detection tailored for agentic transactions using Shared Payment Tokens, with URBN, Etsy, and Coach already onboarding.

title: Under the River
description: Shopify built River, an AI coding agent living in company Slack that reads code, runs tests, and opens pull requests. One in eight merged PRs at Shopify is now coauthored by River. The post details Aquifer, the internal agent platform beneath it, treating session durability as the core primitive.

title: Building production AI agents on Elasticsearch: 5 key lessons
description: Elastic's Field Technology team shares insights from operating AI agents that processed over one million messages in a year. Key finding: retrieval relevance and interaction log analysis matter more than model selection for production agent quality.

title: Effective context engineering for AI agents
description: Anthropic shares principles for building effective AI agents, focusing on context engineering — the practice of providing agents with the right information, tools, and instructions to produce reliable results. Covers prompt design, tool definitions, and managing long-running agent sessions.

title: Supercharging the ML and AI Development Experience at Netflix
description: Netflix details how they enhanced developer experience for ML and AI workflows using Maestro, their workflow orchestrator handling hundreds of thousands of workflows daily. The post covers agent-driven architecture where specialized agents own specific business problems atop a robust foundational platform.

title: Building enterprise AI agents at Coinbase: engineering for trust, scale, and repeatability
description: Coinbase's Agentic AI Tiger Team built paved roads for AI agents in six weeks, putting two automations into production saving 25+ hours per week. Their code-first approach cut new agent build time from 12+ weeks to under a week while upskilling engineers to self-serve.

title: Empowering Engineers with AI
description: Slack describes deploying AI-powered developer tools that save 10,000+ hours of engineering time yearly. Their internal AI assistant has been generally available for five months with 4,000+ monthly chat-turns and 70%+ positive satisfaction across the engineering organization.
